{"step_by_step_thinking": "The crossed extensor reflex is described in Document [13], which states that it is used in the extremities when muscle damage occurs. It involves voluntary contraction inhibiting the same contralateral muscle and activating the contralateral antagonist muscle. This description aligns with the mechanism of a postural reflex, which helps maintain posture and balance. There is no mention of it being a withdrawal reflex, a sympathetic reflex, or a monosynaptic reflex.", "answer_choice": "B"}
